_meta.source: Figure 2, Sainte-Fare-Garnot & Landrieu (2021), PASTIS Dataset Documentation
_meta.nomenclature: 20 semantic classes: background (0) + 18 crop types (1-18) + void (19)
_meta.raw_source: Derived from FLPIS (French Land Parcel Identification System), 73 raw crop codes collapsed into 18 classes with at least 400 parcels and presence in at least 2 of 4 Sentinel-2 tiles
_meta.void_definition: Parcels whose crop type is not in the 18-class nomenclature, or whose overlap with the patch is too small (surface < 50%)
_meta.background_definition: Pixels not within any declared parcel (non-agricultural land uses, 'stuff')
_meta.cmap: matplotlib tab20 (cells 1..19), black for 0 (background), white for 20 (reserved/unused)

19.name: Void label
19.notes: Out-of-scope parcels (crop type not in nomenclature, or <50% overlap with patch)

_groupings_meta.rationale: FLPIS publishes 73 raw codes but the authors filtered to 18 (>=400 parcels AND present in >=2 of 4 tiles). The 55 discarded codes were collapsed into 'void' without preserving their original code, so no finer granularity is recoverable from TARGET_*.npy alone (would require re-joining with the raw IGN FLPIS shapefile geographically). These derived groupings are agronomically motivated VIEWS over the 18 canonical classes, useful for EDA when 18 classes saturate t-SNE/UMAP visualizations or when the question of interest is at a higher abstraction level.

phenological_cycle.description: 4 super-classes by phenological cycle. Useful to ask: does AlphaEarth / Sentinel-2 separate by growing cycle?

agronomic_group.description: 5 super-classes by agronomic / commercial use. Useful for baseline US-017 to evaluate whether the model fails on 18 classes but succeeds on agronomic groups.

cereals_winter_vs_spring.description: Binary view restricted to cereal classes (9 classes -> 2 groups). Useful for AC-7 US-012 (DTW mono- vs double-cycle).
cereals_winter_vs_spring.notes: Sorghum (18) excluded from this view because it is a summer cereal but not directly comparable to spring/winter in the same axis.
